action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 22's ensemble of 4 PyTorch models yielded ic_mean=0.02036, slightly below the single-model best of 0.02045. The new models overfit the 2021 validation period. Analysis: We need a model with a different inductive bias. LightGBM is typically very strong on tabular financial features, but our previous attempts were severely underfit (≤26 trees). A properly tuned LGB (thousands of trees, large num_leaves, regularization) could significantly outperform the MLP. This iteration will train a high-capacity LGB on standardized Alpha158 features and test it locally before any official submission. Decision: (1) Remove the weak ensemble manifest so the main directory falls back to best_512.pt. (2) Write a robust train_lgb.py with strong hyperparameters and early stopping. (3) Update predict.py to support loading a LightGBM Booster from lgb_model.txt. (4) Train LGB, then run predict.py locally against local_test_eval to estimate IC. We will only submit officially if the local IC exceeds our 0.02045 baseline.
action.command: rm -f checkpoints/ensemble_models.json && python train_lgb.py && python predict.py --data_path local_test_eval --checkpoint checkpoints/lgb --output local_test_eval/lgb_output.json
